mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical threshold, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with rising residential construction indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns in at least 60% of observations over a 5-year backtest, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, rising inflation expectations erode real yields and force the Federal Reserve to pause, creating a temporary valuation floor that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 65% of triggered instances during tightening phases, or the inflation-adjusted rate path shows no correlation with equity valuation multiples.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a mechanical positive correlation with forward equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decouples from corporate earnings growth for two consecutive quarters, resulting in negative 5-day forward returns despite positive GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in industrial production above a supply-capacity threshold signals easing supply chain bottlenecks, which compresses input costs and boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative in over 50% of cases where INDPRO crosses 115.0, indicating that capacity expansion does not translate to margin expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with a stable unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor demand rather than cyclical volatility, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined employment-unemployment signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or the cross-product term loses statistical significance in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment, accelerating household credit demand signals peak consumer leverage, which historically precedes earnings revisions downward and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Household credit growth continues to support equity returns for more than two consecutive quarters without triggering a recession or earnings contraction, invalidating the leverage ceiling premise.
